about.title: The Way of Go in the Age of Algorithms
about.introTitle: Prologue: A Sanctuary in the Noise
about.introText1: We live in an era of digital cacophony. Most Go applications today are cluttered with ads and complex features that distract from the simple beauty of the game.
about.introText2: go-daily was built as a sanctuary. By stripping away the noise, we return the focus to the 19x19 grid—a place for true immersion and quiet reflection. Here, there is only you, the shapes, and the fleeting tranquility of placing a stone.
about.section1Text1: The beauty of Go lies not in the final score, but in the quality of thought behind every move.
about.section1Text2: Modern AI is formidable, but its strength can be a double-edged sword. When an algorithm hands you the 'correct' move instantly, it robs you of the struggle that defines true improvement. Knowledge directly handed down is fragile.
about.section1Text3: Insights earned through the 'pain' of deep calculation are the only ones that truly belong to you. Our AI coach is not an oracle of answers—it stays anchored to each puzzle's curated explanation and board context while reviewing with you and nudging you when you hesitate.
about.section2Text1: On March 13, 2016, the world witnessed a moment of pure human transcendence.
about.section2Text2: Against the cold, calculating logic of AlphaGo, Lee Sedol played the 'Divine Move'—a wedge at L11. It was a move the algorithm had deemed a 1-in-10,000 statistical anomaly.
about.section2Text3: In that moment, Lee Sedol proved that even on a finite board, there exists a beauty and spirit that cannot be reduced to binary code or probability.
about.section2Text4: go-daily carries this spirit. We encourage you to seek your own 'Divine Move'—not just the safest play, but the one that resonates with your own intuition and sparks joy in discovery.
about.section3Text1: Now that machines have surpassed us in skill, the meaning of Go has returned to its essence: a path for self-cultivation.
about.section3Text2: From Conquest to Contemplation: Go is no longer a battle to be won against an opponent, but a dialogue with oneself. It is a tool for Inner Peace and a mirror for honing focus and resilience.
about.section3Text3: Symbiosis: We do not compete with machines; we learn from their vastness to expand our own horizons. go-daily is a bridge between human intuition and artificial wisdom—a ladder to higher truths.
about.section4Text1: Hello, I'm Frank, the developer of go-daily.
about.section4Text2: I am a deep Go enthusiast myself, currently at Amateur 3-dan. As a fellow player, I know the frustration of the plateau; as a developer, I am exploring how technology can serve this ancient art.
about.section4Text3: In an age of speed, go-daily is an exercise in idealism. I hope it's a place where you can take five minutes out of a busy day to simply sit down, think deeply, and place one stone with intent.
about.section4Text4: The Way of Go is long, and we are all seekers. I hope this project accompanies you through the alternating seasons of black and white, to see a wider world.
about.footer: (C) 2026 Frank. Dedicated to the beauty of the game.

home.hint: Tap an empty intersection to place a stone
home.boardCoordinateHint: Coordinates use (x,y) with the top-left as (0,0). 19x19 puzzles may show only a local corner.
home.keyboardShortcuts: Shortcuts: Arrow keys move the cursor · Enter or Space places a stone · R resets · Esc clears the current move

onboarding.practiceTitle: Solve one puzzle, then let AI explain why
onboarding.practiceSubtitle: No registration wall and no long tutorial. Pick a starter level, place one move, review the feedback, ask the coach, and turn this session into tomorrow's review.
hints: First identify the target shape: which group needs to live, die, or find a tesuji?
Then inspect liberties and eye shape: which points reduce eye space or keep your follow-up threats alive?
Finally look for the vital point: prefer a move that creates two threats or leaves the opponent with fewer replies.
onboarding.resultTitleCorrect: Good. The first learning loop is complete
onboarding.resultTitleWrong: This is exactly the right puzzle to review
onboarding.resultBodyCorrect: Do not rush to the next puzzle yet. First understand why this move works, then ask AI to turn the shape into a reusable rule of thumb.
onboarding.resultBodyWrong: A mistake is not a failed session; it is the best training sample. Read the key explanation, then ask AI what opportunity your move gave the opponent.

result.coachEmpty: Ask anything — e.g. "Why is this the vital point?" or "What happens if White hanes?"
result.coachLimited: AI coach is available on approved coach-ready puzzles only.
result.coachBasicExplained: This puzzle has a static explanation, but it is not in the AI Coach queue yet.
result.coachEligibleLimited: This puzzle has a basic explanation and can enter the content backfill queue; full AI Coach stays closed until the main line and wrong branches are reviewed.
result.coachReadyBoundary: coach-ready: ask about the main line and common wrong moves.
result.coachVariationReady: variation-ready: supports related shape variations and follow-up questions.
result.coachGuestLocked: Sign in to use AI Coach and sync your progress across devices.
result.coachUsageBilling: {{today}} left today · {{month}} left this billing cycle
result.coachTierBasicExplained: basic-explained: read the curated explanation only.
result.coachTierEligible: coach-eligible: content is waiting for reviewed coach lines.
result.coachErrorTimeout: Coach is taking too long. Please try again.
result.coachErrorRateLimit: Coach is busy. Please try again in a moment.
result.coachErrorAuth: Coach authentication failed. Please contact support.
result.coachErrorTemporary: Coach is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
result.coachErrorEmpty: Coach returned an empty reply. Please try again.
result.coachDeviceLimit: Free accounts work on one device at a time. Upgrade to Pro for multi-device sync and AI Coach everywhere.
result.coachDailyLimitReached: You've used today's AI Coach limit.
result.coachMonthlyLimitReached: You've used this month's AI Coach limit.
result.coachGuestDailyLimit: You've used today's free AI Coach sessions. Sign up for free to get 10 per day.
result.coachGuestMonthlyLimit: You've used this month's free AI Coach sessions. Sign up for free to get 30 per month.
result.explanationCorrectLead: The point is not only that the move is correct; it changes the opponent's next choices.
result.explanationWrongLead: Do not rush the retry. Understanding why this move falls short is what makes the shape recognizable next time.

missed-vital-point.title: Missed vital point
missed-vital-point.mistake: Your move was near the key area, but not on the point that changes liberties, eye shape, or the opponent's reply. Compare the one-point difference with the solution.
missed-vital-point.training: This puzzle trains vital-point recognition: do not stop at the general area; find the move that immediately changes both players' options.

shape-reading.title: Shape reading
shape-reading.mistake: This looks more like an incomplete read of the local shape. Step through the first one or two solution moves, then compare what weakness your move leaves behind.
shape-reading.training: This puzzle trains shape reading: connections, cutting points, sente, and follow-up shape matter more than the point that simply looks largest.

liberty-counting.title: Liberty counting
liberty-counting.mistake: This looks more like a liberty count or move-order miss. Count the liberties of the key groups first, then see how the solution changes who gets squeezed first.
liberty-counting.training: This puzzle trains liberty counting: count the key groups first, then confirm which move changes the race to capture or live.

endgame-value.title: Endgame value
endgame-value.mistake: This looks more like a missed comparison of endgame value and sente. In review, focus on why the solution is more efficient than nearby local plays.
endgame-value.training: This puzzle trains endgame value: compare points, sente/gote, and local finishing efficiency.

opening-direction.title: Opening direction
opening-direction.mistake: This looks more like a direction choice that drifted from the whole-board priority. In review, watch how the solution balances big points, thickness, pressure, and development.
opening-direction.training: This puzzle trains opening direction: use the whole-board relationship to decide whether to approach, expand, press, or turn away.

nextRecommendation.reviewPrompt: {{count}} unresolved mistake(s) are waiting in Review. You can keep practicing now and come back to them next.

onboarding-path.title: Stay on this training path
onboarding-path.body: Keep the same onboarding level for the next puzzle so the difficulty stays stable while you build rhythm.

correct-same-level.title: Continue at this level
correct-same-level.body: You solved it after some resistance, so the next useful step is another puzzle at the same difficulty band.

correct-step-up.title: Try a slightly harder puzzle
correct-step-up.body: This was a clean solve. Move one small step up in difficulty while the pattern is still fresh.

wrong-same-mistake.title: Practice this mistake pattern again
wrong-same-mistake.body: The next puzzle will stay close to this difficulty and topic, so you can test the same reading habit immediately.

wrong-same-topic.title: Repeat the same topic
wrong-same-topic.body: Stay near this topic and difficulty before moving on. One more nearby problem is more useful than jumping around.

fallback-practice.title: Keep practicing from here
fallback-practice.body: There is not enough history yet, so the next puzzle stays near the current difficulty.

result.saveBodyGuest: Guest progress is saved on this device. Sign in to sync mistakes, preserve the review plan, and continue elsewhere.
result.saveBodySignedIn: This attempt is in your training history. Missed puzzles will appear in Review and the spaced practice path.
result.coachPromptMainLine: What is the main line after the correct move?
result.coachPromptWhyWrong: Why was my move not good enough?
result.coachPromptCorrectLine: What is the continuation after the correct move?
result.coachPromptPattern: How do I recognize this shape next time?
result.coachPromptNextJudgment: How should I judge this next time?
result.coachPromptBasicExplanation: What should I focus on in the explanation?
result.coachPromptReviewNote: What needs to be reviewed before full Coach opens?
result.boardCoordinateHint: The AI coach uses the same (x,y) coordinates as the page, with the top-left as (0,0).
result.keyboardShortcuts: Shortcuts: R retries · Left and right arrows step through the solution · Esc closes the current solution view

stats.title: My Stats
stats.empty: No records yet. Solve your first puzzle!
stats.backupDescription: Export your records to a JSON file or import them on another device.

insights.title: Training focus
insights.noMistakeReasons: Likely mistake habits will appear after more review.
insights.noTrend: No practice in the last 7 days.

mentors.title: Meet Your Mentors
mentors.description: Every puzzle on go-daily is analyzed by top-tier Go engines. Our AI mentors bring that analysis to life through their unique personas and coaching styles.

review.title: Review
review.subtitle: Puzzles you haven't cracked yet
review.empty: No mistakes to review — keep it up!
review.srsSubtitle: Pro spaced review: puzzles due today
review.emptySrs: No review cards are due today.
review.freeLimitNotice: Free review shows the latest {{limit}} puzzles. Pro SRS can manage {{count}} more.

insights.title: Today's review summary
insights.focusWithTag: Start with {{tag}} because your unresolved mistakes cluster there.
insights.focusBacklog: Clear the waiting review queue before jumping to harder puzzles.
insights.focusClear: No review pressure right now. Continue with a fresh puzzle.
insights.whySrs: Based on due SRS cards and your latest saved attempts.
insights.whyLocal: Based on unresolved mistakes saved on this device.

hero.subtitle: Experience the silent battle of the ancient game. Hone your skill with every stone, and feel the wisdom of millennia unfold.

boardShowcase.title: Move 78
boardShowcase.subtitle: The Divine Move
boardShowcase.description: In the fourth game of the historic match, Lee Sedol played the legendary "wedge" at L11 — a move so unexpected that it forced AlphaGo into a series of errors, leading to humanity's only victory in the series.

manifest.name: go-daily — Daily Go Puzzle
manifest.description: One Go puzzle a day with DeepSeek-backed AI coaching.

home.title: go-daily — Daily Go Puzzle with AI Coach
home.description: Master Go (Weiqi/Baduk) with one hand-picked puzzle a day. AI coaching stays aligned with official explanations—helping you understand the 'why', not just the 'where'.

today.title: Today's Puzzle — go-daily
today.description: Challenge yourself with today's hand-picked Go puzzle and improve your reading with AI coaching.

onboarding.title: First Training Session — go-daily
onboarding.description: Complete your first Go puzzle in 3 minutes: play a move, get feedback, let AI explain why, and save the review path.

puzzles.title: Puzzle Library — go-daily
puzzles.description: Browse hundreds of life-and-death, tesuji, and endgame puzzles. Filter by difficulty and track your progress.

review.title: Review Your Mistakes — go-daily
review.description: Review the puzzles you've missed and master the shapes you find difficult.

stats.title: Your Progress & Stats — go-daily
